prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a3f7c1d9e42b8056","BT":2,"BW":3,"HF":0.42,"RF":0.31,"MA":2,"hour_entropy":2.18,"behavioral_consistency":0.31,"response_latency_variance":6.92,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.74,"error_recovery_pattern":0.062,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.77,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.38,"gas_price_precision":0.64,"rationale":"Seed-1003 personality models a lower-entropy, somewhat habitual human who uses round gas tips and has erratic inter-tx timing (high latency variance), placing all 13 features within 1-sigma of the human mean while staying strictly below every HasciDB flag threshold."}
